CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the Quality Information Document (QuID) is updated, and a 
row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document . Column 5 should include the names of the author (s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means th e 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

4.0 
 
                                                      Page 4/ 41 
KEY DEFINITIONS  
Analysis Incorporation of an observation data assimilation step  to a model 
prediction system in order to achieve an optimal estimate of the true state 
of the atmospheric or marine system at a given point in time.  
Average zero -upcrossing 
period  
(model VTM02) 
Average time interval between consecutive waves as they cross the mean 
sea level in an upward direction. 
Average wave direction  
(model VMDR) 
Average direction of wave approach in a given sea state. 
Coastal Waters Approximately 5  km or less from the coastline and where bathymetric 
variability is less than a kilometre in scale. 
Forecast A simulation that uses a numerical model to predict a future 
period (typically days-weeks in the future) 
Hindcast A simulation that uses a numerical model to recreate a historical 
period (typically days-decades in the past) 
Match-up Co-location in space and time between a model prediction and a real-world 
measurement. 
Open Waters Approximately 5 km or more from the coastline and where bathymetric 
variability is on the scale of a kilometre or greater. 
Peak wave period  
(model VTPK) 
Interval in seconds between wave crests associated with the peak wave 
energy frequency. 
Significant wave height 
(model VHM0) 
Average height of the largest 1/3rd of waves in a given sea state. Computed 
using sea surface elevations (four times the standard deviation of the 
surface elevation) or from the zeroth-order moment of the wave spectrum 
from a wave model.
